questions.question: Explain De Casteljau's algorithm for evaluating a Bezier curve at parameter t=0.5. Given control points P0(0,0), P1(2,4), P2(4,2), P3(6,0), calculate the intermediate points at one iteration.
keyPoints: De Casteljau's algorithm uses linear interpolation between control points
Formula: Pi(t) = (1-t)Pi + tPi+1 for successive iterations
First iteration computes 3 intermediate points from 4 control points
At t=0.5, intermediate points are Q0(1,2), Q1(3,3), Q2(5,1)

questions.question: Define Bernstein basis polynomials of degree n and state two properties that make them suitable for Bezier curve representation.
keyPoints: Properties: Partition of unity (sum equals 1) and non-negativity for t in [0,1]
They form a basis for polynomials of degree n
Control points are weighted by these polynomials to form Bezier curve

questions.question: Given a cubic Hermite curve defined by endpoints P0(1,2) and P3(5,6) with tangent vectors m0(1,0) and m1(1,0), derive the equivalent cubic Bezier control points. Also write the Hermite basis matrix and explain how to convert between the two representations.
keyPoints: Hermite to Bezier: P1 = P0 + m0/3 and P2 = P3 - m1/3
Calculated control points: P1(1.33,2), P2(4.67,6), P3(5,6)
Bezier basis uses Bernstein polynomials: combines control points via B(i,3,t)

questions.question: Write a complete algorithm in pseudocode to subdivide a cubic Bezier curve at parameter t=0.5 using the de Casteljau method. Explain how this algorithm demonstrates the convex hull property and discuss the computational complexity of evaluating a Bezier curve at a parameter value.
keyPoints: Algorithm: Repeated linear interpolation creating triangle of points at each level
At t=0.5: Level 0 has 4 points, Level 1 has 3 points, Level 2 has 2 points, Level 3 has 1 point (curve point)
Convex hull property: Entire curve lies inside polygon formed by control points, proven by iterative interpolation
Complexity: O(n²) for degree n, or O(n) using Horner's method with Bernstein polynomials
